ecosystems.cloudNotes: Apex Fusion is free for all Apex owners. No paid tier as of this dataset.
ecosystems.programmingModel: Apex programming language — If-This-Then-That style rule engine with virtual outlets and real scripting depth. GUI wizards cover common tasks; power users write code strings.
ecosystems.appNotes: Apex Fusion iOS: 3.4/5 from 128 ratings — dated UX is the common complaint.
strengths: Largest install base and third-party ecosystem — most gear brands integrate with Apex first
Trident is the most mature automated Alk/Ca/Mg tester on the market
Deepest used market and community knowledge base (Reef2Reef)
Free cloud (Apex Fusion) with hardwired Ethernet fallback on full-size units
weaknesses: WiFi reliability is the #1 owner complaint — drop-offs, WPA3 trouble; many owners hardwire Ethernet
Fusion app UX widely criticized as dated (3.4/5 App Store)
Trident needs ~$45 reagent refills every 2 months — a real recurring cost
ecosystems.testing: Trident automated Alk/Ca/Mg tester — $779.99 (Trident NP adds NO3/PO4). Reagent-based: ~$45 refill kit every ~2 months.
ecosystems.dosing: DOS QuietDrive dual-head dosing system — $374.99, native Apex/Fusion integration.

kits.outletsNotes: Energy Bar 832: 8 AC outlets + 3 1LINK + 2 24VDC accessory ports; 4× 0-10V variable outputs; leak + level sensors included
Energy Bar 832 included; no 0-10V outputs or ORP probe vs Pro
Monitoring-focused entry unit — no Energy Bar included; add one later for outlet control

ecosystems.cloudNotes: HYDROS app is free; no paid tier found. Bluetooth local fallback exists but is more limited than Apex's local page.
ecosystems.programmingModel: Collectives — GUI automation builder, closest to smart-home app logic. Easiest of the three; no scripting layer.
ecosystems.appNotes: HYDROS iOS app: 3.9/5 from just 18 ratings — small sample, newest ecosystem.
strengths: Distributed mesh architecture — brain and outlet units are separate, so one failure doesn't take down the system
Native wave-pump control (WaveEngine LE, $248.31) — no third-party bolt-on needed
Cheapest real entry: Control X2 at $198.65, Start kit at $346.49
Simplest programming model — fastest path to a working setup
weaknesses: Newest ecosystem: smallest community, thinnest third-party integration list
WiFi power strip is the flaky link — owners report daily connect/disconnect alert flapping
Some reports of manual power-cycling needed after outages
No automated water tester (no Trident / ION Director equivalent) — a real capability gap
Heavy SKU churn — several starter packs already retired on CoralVue's own site
ecosystems.testing: None — no automated water-testing device in the HYDROS line as of this dataset.
ecosystems.dosing: HYDROS dosing pump — $72.75 per pump, needs a free Drive Port on a Control unit. Modular per-pump pricing vs Neptune's all-in-one DOS.

kits.outletsNotes: X4 brain + XP8 8-outlet powerbar with per-outlet power monitoring + pH probe & calibration kit
X3 brain + XP8 powerbar; probe contents not itemized on the listing
Entry bundle below the Pro Packs; contents not fully itemized on the listing
Bare brain: 2 Drive Ports + 2 Sense Ports, no probes, no AC outlet control without a WiFi power strip

ecosystems.cloudNotes: myGHL / GHL Connect cloud is free. Next-gen 'GHL Connect AI' announced for late 2026, pricing undisclosed.
ecosystems.programmingModel: Full logic-gate programming (AND/OR/NOT chains) — the most powerful and the steepest learning curve of the three. Optional Control Pad touchscreen ($249.90) for daily use without programming.
ecosystems.appNotes: GHL Connect iOS: 4.0/5 from only 4 ratings — sample too small to mean anything.
strengths: Most complete out-of-box probe set: Mega-Set ships lab-grade pH, ORP, conductivity/salinity AND temp probes with calibration fluids
Most powerful programming logic of the three for power users
ION Director tests Ca/Mg/K/Na/NO3 with ion-selective electrodes — NO consumable reagents, unlike Trident
weaknesses: Steepest learning curve — Apex switchers say you have to 'forget the Apex mindset'
Companion software (GCC) described as crash-prone and slow by owners
Customer service criticized for templated troubleshooting
Weakest US retail footprint — thin coverage at specialty retailers like BRS vs Neptune/CoralVue; GHL's own US storefront is the reliable price source
ecosystems.testing: ION Director — reagent-free Ca/Mg/K/Na/NO3 via ion-selective electrodes; sold in US bundles with a GHL Doser from ~$1,034.
ecosystems.dosing: GHL Doser 3 (EXT/standalone) — bundled with ProfiLux/ION Director from ~$1,020; standalone US pricing not cleanly published.

kits.outletsNotes: Powerbar 6E-PAB (6 controllable AC outlets) + lab-grade probe set with full calibration fluids — the most complete kit contents in this comparison

ecosystems.cloudNotes: Reef Angel app (iOS/Android) is free, no paid tier — remote monitoring, control, and push/email alerts.
ecosystems.programmingModel: Open-source Arduino (ATmega) platform — a setup wizard auto-generates code for common automation, and the full Arduino sketch is yours to edit for anything the wizard doesn't cover.
ecosystems.programmingDifficulty: Wizard-easy for the basics; genuinely technical (Arduino/C++) once you go past it
ecosystems.appNotes: Reef Angel app handles remote monitoring, control, and alerts — owners consistently describe it as more utilitarian than Fusion/myGHL/HYDROS.
strengths: Cheapest complete kit on this page — $399 includes the relay box, probes, and float switches with nothing extra required to get running
Fully open-source (Arduino) — public source code and hardware schematics, a real point of difference from every closed ecosystem above
Built-in WiFi on the Star — the earlier RA+ needed a separate add-on module
Actively maintained — GitHub commits into December 2025, still a going concern (unlike some DIY-era peers, see ReefKeeper below)
weaknesses: Smallest third-party ecosystem here — no automated water-testing hardware exists for it (no Trident/ION Director equivalent)
No native dosing pump — relay outlets can switch a third-party doser on/off but can't program a dose volume
Full customization requires real Arduino/C++ comfort — the wizard covers the basics, but the 'open and hackable' pitch cuts both ways
Smallest community and support footprint of any current (non-discontinued) option on this page
ecosystems.testing: No automated water-testing hardware in the Reef Angel ecosystem — pair a manual test kit and log results in the app.
ecosystems.dosing: No native dosing pump. Relay outlets can switch a third-party doser on/off only — for programmable dose volumes, pair it with a standalone doser (see our dosing pump review).

kits.outletsNotes: 8-port relay box + 2 float-switch inputs included; 3 temperature channels total (1 probe in the box, more sold separately)

reefPi.summary: Open-source Raspberry Pi controller (actively maintained, May 2026 commits). Automates ATO, temp, lighting, dosing, pH for roughly $150 in parts — zero warranty, zero support, real time investment.
